A developer has raised this issue: "Implement consumer group rebalance protocol with incremental partition assignment"
Domain: distributed-systems | Complexity: high

Break this down into a well-structured task with full project management detail.

Return JSON with this exact structure:
{
  "task_id": "TASK-XXX",
  "title": "short action-verb title",
  "description": "2-3 sentence detailed description",
  "acceptance_criteria": ["criterion 1", "criterion 2", "criterion 3"],
  "story_points": <fibonacci: 1,2,3,5,8,13>,
  "priority": "Critical|High|Medium|Low",
  "labels": ["label1", "label2"],
  "dependencies": ["describe any blocking tasks or leave empty array"],
  "definition_of_done": "clear measurable done state"
}

{
  "task_id": "TASK-789",
  "title": "Implement Incremental Partition Assignment",
  "description": "This task implements a consumer group rebalance protocol that supports incremental partition assignment. This will reduce the disruption to consumers during rebalances by assigning partitions in smaller batches, improving overall consumer experience and reducing latency spikes. The implementation will focus on minimizing the time consumers are offline during the rebalance process.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Rebalance completes with incremental partition assignment without consumer data loss.",
    "Rebalance time is reduced by at least 20% compared to the current full assignment approach for groups with >10 partitions and >5 consumers (measured in integration tests).",
    "New protocol is backwards compatible with existing consumers and brokers (verified through compatibility tests).",
    "Comprehensive unit and integration tests cover all code paths, including failure scenarios."
  ],
  "story_points": 13,
  "priority": "High",
  "labels": [
    "kafka-improvement",
    "consumer",
    "rebalance",
    "distributed-systems"
  ],
  "dependencies": [
    "TASK-788: Design incremental partition assignment protocol"
  ],
  "definition_of_done": "Code is reviewed and merged into the main branch, all unit and integration tests pass with 100% coverage, performance benchmarks meet the acceptance criteria, and documentation is updated to reflect the new functionality."
}

{
  "task_id": "TASK-1247",
  "title": "Implement Delta Lake ACID Transactions for Structured Streaming",
  "description": "This task focuses on enabling ACID transaction guarantees when writing Structured Streaming outputs to Delta Lake. This will ensure data consistency and reliability in streaming pipelines, particularly during failures or concurrent writes. The implementation will involve integrating Delta Lake's transaction log with the Structured Streaming engine.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Structured Streaming jobs can successfully write data to Delta Lake tables with full ACID transaction support (commit, rollback, isolation).",
    "Concurrent Structured Streaming jobs writing to the same Delta Lake table do not result in data corruption or inconsistencies.",
    "Failure scenarios (e.g., driver crashes, executor failures) during Structured Streaming jobs result in proper transaction rollback and data integrity in Delta Lake.",
    "Performance overhead introduced by ACID transactions is within acceptable limits (defined as less than 15% increase in end-to-end latency).",
    "Comprehensive unit and integration tests cover various failure scenarios and concurrent write conditions."
  ],
  "story_points": 13,
  "priority": "High",
  "labels": [
    "spark",
    "structured-streaming",
    "delta-lake",
    "acid-transactions",
    "data-reliability"
  ],
  "dependencies": [
    "TASK-1245: Update Delta Lake connector to latest version with transaction log API",
    "TASK-1246: Design document outlining transaction coordination strategy within Structured Streaming"
  ],
  "definition_of_done": "Code is merged into the main branch, all acceptance criteria are met, comprehensive unit and integration tests pass with 95% code coverage, performance benchmarks are documented, and the feature is documented in the Spark documentation."
}
